evidence: docs/features/FEAT-0002-test-architecture-capability/README.md records reviewed topology, traceability, discovery, process, and fixture evidence.
tests/Verify-MeAndAIAdoption.ps1 recursively discovers and executes capability suites in ordinal separate processes.
tests/capabilities/test-architecture/test-architecture.tests.ps1 validates topology, process isolation, cleanup, and terminal evidence.

Reviewed tests/Verify-MeAndAIAdoption.ps1 and the three capability suites: validation uses small isolated PowerShell processes, short read-only Git metadata queries, one resolver idempotence rerun, fresh empty temporary roots, and in-memory fixtures; it provisions no reusable repository, archive, container, or service setup.

The managed lifecycle workflow repository checkouts and GitHub calls are operational trust boundaries with distinct job authorities, not repeated project-test fixtures; reusable updater concerns remain owned by the pinned common implementation.

The retained child-process boundary supplies suite-isolation and discovery evidence; sharing it would remove required evidence, while fixture reuse or operation-budget machinery would provide no material reliability or runner-consumption benefit.

.github/scripts/Invoke-MeAndAIProtocolUpdate.ps1 reads migration baselines from exact regular HEAD blobs, uses the filtered worktree only as a drift check, and validates candidate results from exact stage-zero index blobs before commit.

.github/workflows/meandai-protocol-update.yml binds the checked-out consumer head to the immutable event and delegates capability-ledger resolution to the review runner from the pinned protocol checkout.

The pinned MeAndAI.RepositoryEvidence boundary selects exact HEAD, index, or contained ordinary worktree bytes, fails closed on ambiguous state, and its release-declared anonymous-Git evidence covers checkout filtering, exact-byte rejection, containment, read-only behavior, and idempotent reruns.

tests/capabilities/canonical-repository-evidence/canonical-repository-evidence.tests.ps1 proves strict ledger parsing through the pinned resolver, idempotent source selection, and exact managed-projection blob mapping.
